title: Constraint–Market Compatibility — NAICS 493120 Refrigerated Warehousing and Storage

sections.title: Market operating envelope (from 05 + VN)
sections.content:
| Dimension | Cold storage reality |
|---|---|
| Site type | Large industrial warehouse with existing MV feeder |
| Typical load | 24/7 compressor + chamber HVAC, multi-hundred-kW continuous |
| Grid regime | VDE-AR-N 4110 / FGW TR8 (same as Mein Solar reference) |
| Ambient | German outdoor, typically –15 °C winter low to +35 °C summer peak |
| BESS siting | Outdoor, container footprint, separate from cold chamber envelope |
| Business case | Peak-shaving of continuous refrigeration load + spoilage resilience |
| Investor profile | Logistics operator (Nordfrost, Kühne+Nagel), payback-driven |

---

sections.title: Verdict summary
sections.content:
| # | Constraint | Type | Is abs. | Verdict | Cost | Time |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | MV Grid Connection Capacity | operational | false | **mitigable** | medium | 3–9 mo |
| 2 | Land Footprint & Siting | physical | false | **none** | — | — |
| 3 | Fire-Protection Clearance | regulatory | false | **mitigable** | low | 2–4 mo |
| 4 | Li-Ion Thermal Runaway Hazard | physical | false | **mitigable** | low | 1–3 mo |
| 5 | Grid-Code Certification Lead Time | regulatory | false | **mitigable** | medium | 6–12 mo |
| 6 | Battery Supply-Chain Concentration | economic | false | **none** | — | — |
| 7 | Ambient Temperature Envelope | environmental | false | **none** | — | — |
| 8 | Round-Trip Energy Losses | physical | true | **none** | — | — |
| 9 | CAPEX Viability Threshold | economic | false | **mitigable** | medium | 2–6 mo |

**Knockouts: 0** → Market **SURVIVES**.

---

sections.title: Per-constraint rationale
sections.content:
### C1 — MV Grid Connection Capacity → mitigable
Cold stores already draw continuous multi-hundred-kW refrigeration load over a dedicated MV feeder; spare capacity for charge-window dispatch is the question, not presence of MV. Where feeder headroom is tight, BESS sizing can be load-shifted to off-peak charging windows, or a minor DSO upgrade negotiated. No physics block.
**Mitigation:** DSO headroom study + EMS charge-window dispatch logic + optional feeder upgrade.

### C2 — Land Footprint & Siting → none
40–80 m²/MWh is trivial on an industrial cold-storage site that already houses 5,000–30,000 m² warehouse plus truck apron and refrigeration plant. Perimeter or apron-adjacent siting is standard. Baugenehmigung path is the same as adding any outdoor utility plant.

### C3 — Fire-Protection Clearance → mitigable
VdS / Feuerwehr clearance distances (separation walls, hydrant access, >3 MWh cluster segmentation) apply identically to every DE industrial site. Cold-store operators already run a fire-safety regime for refrigeration (NH₃/CO₂ detection, sprinklers); adding BESS-specific clearance to an outdoor site is routine.
**Mitigation:** Early Feuerwehr-Abstimmung, cluster segmentation >3 MWh, standard fire-protection concept.

### C4 — Li-Ion Thermal Runaway Hazard → mitigable
Hazard is a design constraint, not a market-specific block. Containerised BESS with NOVEC/aerosol suppression, deflagration venting, and compartmentalisation is the standard answer everywhere Mein Solar builds. No additional cold-store-specific adaptation required — BESS sits outside the refrigerated envelope.
**Mitigation:** Standard containerised design with VdS-aligned suppression.

### C5 — Grid-Code Certification Lead Time → mitigable
6–12 month FGW TR8 / VDE-AR-N 4110 path is identical to every German MV-connected asset Mein Solar delivers. Not market-specific. Parallelisable with procurement and civil works.
**Mitigation:** Early DSO Netzanschlussanfrage + parallel Type-B plant-cert dossier prep.

### C6 — Battery Supply-Chain Concentration → none
LFP sourcing risk (Chinese concentration, CRMA, EU Battery Reg. compliance) is product-inherent and identical across every market Mein Solar enters. Cold storage adds no incremental exposure. Handled at portfolio-level procurement.

### C7 — Ambient Temperature Envelope → none
–20 to +50 °C internal envelope is maintained by container HVAC regardless of market. German outdoor ambient (–15 to +35 °C typical) is well within limits. Cold-storage facilities are typically in industrial zones with normal outdoor climate (the freezing is *inside* the warehouse, not at the BESS container pad).

### C8 — Round-Trip Energy Losses → none
10–15 % AC-AC loss is a thermodynamic floor that applies to every BESS deployment. Cold-storage business case (peak-shaving a €/kWh spread of 15–25 ct/kWh between off-peak and on-peak tariff, plus Netzentgelte reduction via §19 StromNEV) easily accommodates this floor. No market-specific issue.

### C9 — CAPEX Viability Threshold → mitigable
€250–400/kWh threshold is the generic BESS business-case floor. Cold storage's 24/7 continuous refrigeration load is an unusually *good* peak-shaving target (high load factor → high energy throughput → short payback), but asset-owner awareness is low (adoption = emerging). The mitigation is commercial, not technical.
**Mitigation:** Education-led sale with quantified peak-shaving + Netzentgelte + resilience business case; optional KfW 295 / 296 financing; BESS-as-a-Service structure to shift capex off customer balance sheet.

---

sections.title: Architecture-distance implications (cross-ref to 05 scoring)
sections.content:
| 05 scoring criterion | Constraint link | Implication |
|---|---|---|
| U1 Operating env. (3) | C7 ambient | Matches — outdoor industrial pad, standard climate |
| C1 Install match (4) | C1 MV, C2 land | Both routine at this site type |
| C2 Operator skill (3) | C5 cert, C4 safety | Cold-store has MV + fire-safety operations already |
| C4 Funding (5) | C9 capex | Weakest score — no revenue-stacking culture, education-led sale |

The single 5-score in 05 (C4 funding model) aligns with the only commercial mitigation (C9 CAPEX) — a sales-effort problem, not a physics problem.

---

Five mitigable (MV headroom, fire clearance, thermal-runaway design, grid-code lead time, capex business case) plus four irrelevant (land, supply chain, ambient, round-trip losses). All mitigations are standard Mein Solar EPC deliverables or commercial sales-effort items. The weakest dimension (C4 funding model, score 5 in 05) aligns with C9 CAPEX mitigation — an education-and-financing gap, not a technical barrier.
